1.note: In the hope of eternal life, which God, who does not lie, promised — Numbers 23:19 (God is not a man that he should lie, nor a son of man that he should change his mind) is the OT ground for Paul's affirmation of God's non-deceptive character; the promise of eternal life is guaranteed by the divine truthfulness that distinguishes YHWH from the lying gods of the nations

Promised before the beginning of time (pro chronōn aiōniōn) — Isaiah 40:28 (the LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth) grounds the pre-temporal promise in the divine eternity; the God who exists before all time can make promises that precede the world's creation

3.note: At his appointed season he brought it to light through the preaching — Isaiah 49:8 (in the time of my favor I will answer you, and in the day of salvation I will help you) is the OT model of the kairological promise: God acts at the appointed time. Paul applies the Isaianic appointed-time language to the Christ-event as the fulfillment of the pre-temporal promise

Through the preaching (kērygma) entrusted to me — Isaiah 52:7 (how beautiful are the feet of him who brings good news) is the OT ground for the herald-proclamation; Paul's stewardship of the kērygma is his participation in the Isaianic messenger who announces YHWH's reign

9.note: He must hold firmly to the trustworthy message... so that he can encourage others by sound doctrine and refute those who oppose it — the watchman commission in Ezekiel 33:7-9 (Son of man, I have made you a watchman; if you do not warn the wicked from their way, their blood I will require at your hand) provides the OT model for the overseer's duty to teach sound doctrine and refute error: pastoral leadership is a watchman function

The lips of a priest should preserve knowledge, and people should seek instruction from his mouth (Mal 2:7) — the priestly-teaching mandate is the OT background for the overseer's requirement to both encourage by sound doctrine and refute those who contradict

14.note: Pay no attention to human commands of those who reject the truth — Isaiah 29:13 (their worship of me is made up only of rules taught by men, cited by Jesus in Matt 15:9) is the OT diagnostic for substituting human religious tradition for genuine encounter with God; Paul's phrase echoes the Isaianic critique of a religion that follows human commands rather than divine truth

The merely human commands (entolais anthrōpōn) who reject the truth — Colossians 2:22 applies the same Isaiah 29:13 language to the Colossian ascetic false teachers; the parallel application to Titus's Cretan false teachers shows that the same Isaianic diagnostic operates across Paul's different contexts of false teaching

15.note: To the pure, all things are pure — Genesis 1:31 (God saw all that he had made, and it was very good) is the creation-theology ground of the purity principle; what God created good is not inherently defiling. The Mosaic clean/unclean distinction is an overlay on the creation's fundamental goodness; for those renewed in Christ, the creation's original purity is restored

But to those who are corrupted and do not believe, nothing is pure — Haggai 2:11-13 explores the contagion logic: holiness is not contagious but uncleanness is. The corrupted person defiles whatever they touch. Paul applies the inverse: the pure person has the purifying disposition that sees creation's goodness; the corrupted person's impurity taints their perception of everything

16.note: They claim to know God but by their actions they deny him — Jeremiah 2:8 (the priests did not say, Where is the LORD? Those who handle the law did not know me) is the OT archetype of religious professionals who claim covenant knowledge while practicing covenant violation; Paul applies the same diagnostic to the Cretan false teachers whose doctrinal claim outstrips their moral practice

Detestable and disobedient and unfit for any good work — Amos 5:21-24 (I hate and despise your religious festivals; your offerings I will not accept) is the prophetic ground for describing insincere religion as detestable (the same word) to God; the prophetic tradition consistently treats the gap between professed devotion and lived practice as a primary form of covenant breach

14.note: To purify for himself a people of his own possession — YHWH's Sinai declaration 'you shall be my treasured possession' (LXX: laos periousios, exactly the term used here); the new covenant community is the fulfillment of what the Sinai covenant pointed toward
Who gave himself to redeem us from all lawlessness — YHWH's promise to cleanse Israel from all their backslidings; the redemption from lawlessness fulfills Ezekiel's new covenant cleansing promise
He will redeem Israel from all iniquity -- Jesus redeems a pure people

5.note: Renewing of the Holy Spirit whom he poured out on us richly — YHWH's promise to sprinkle clean water, put his Spirit within, and cause Israel to walk in his statutes; the new birth through the Spirit fulfills Ezekiel's new covenant restoration
